responsivity.evidence: The highest responsivity was 0.31 and 0.58 A W −1 at 78 K and 130 K, respectively.

The speciﬁc detectivity of the CTAB-treated device is given by D /C3 ¼ R ﬃﬃﬃ ﬃ A p =N ð3Þ where N is the spectral noise density (0.5 pA Hz−1/2), R is the responsivity (0.58 A W−1), and A is the area of the detector (1.05 mm 2).
Also, with increasing the silver doping ratio, the p-type character of Ag-HgTe NCs was enhanced, and the responsivity reached 0.58 A W -1 at 130 K.

papers.title: Heavy Metal Free Ag2Se Quantum Dot Inks for Near to Short-Wave Infrared Detection
papers.sourceFile: /Users/shlokp/Zotero/storage/BA3Z4Q9Q/Paul et al. - 2025 - Heavy Metal Free Ag2Se Quantum Dot Inks for Near to Short-Wave Infrared Detection.pdf

responsivity.evidence: The SPLE-based photoconductors exhibit responsivities of ∼150 mA/W under a 0.3 V bias in the 800−1250 nm range.

reported a photodiode exhibiting a responsivity of 4.17 mA/W under reverse bias, although key performance metrics such as detectivity were not disclosed and the overall characterization remained limited.
Their devices achieved responsivities of approximately 3 mA/W under a 5 V bias, with a detectivity of 7 × 10 9 Jones estimated using the shot noise approximation.

36−38 In this work, we first demonstrate a simple photoconductor with a responsivity around 150 mA/W under a 0.3 V bias, marking the highest reported performance for an Ag 2 Se-based photoconductor in the 800−1250 nm range.
The responsivity qualitatively follows the trend in optical absorption with a responsivity value of around 150 mA/W at 1200 nm corresponding to the first excitonic peak of the cQDs.

32 The most relevant comparison is to earlier work from our group employing a solid-state ligand exchanged Ag 2 Se film with 1,2- ethanedithiol ligands, which exhibited responsivities of ∼25 mA/W at similar biases.
To evaluate statistical reproducibility, we measured ten nominally identical devices at 1200 nm shown in Figure 3d, observing a mean responsivity of 142 mA/W with a standard deviation of 4.7 mA/W.
To evaluate statistical reproducibility, we measured ten nominally identical devices at 1200 nm shown in Figure 3d, observing a mean responsivity of 142 mA/W with a standard deviation of 4.7 mA/W.
The obtained responsivity of ∼70 mA/W at 1200 nm, 140 Hz and 0 V bias is far superior to previous reports for Ag 2 Se in the NIR ranging around 1−4 mA/W measured at lower chopping frequencies.
The obtained responsivity of ∼70 mA/W at 1200 nm, 140 Hz and 0 V bias is far superior to previous reports for Ag 2 Se in the NIR ranging around 1−4 mA/W measured at lower chopping frequencies.

The resultant devices set new performance benchmarks for Ag 2 Se-based systems, with photoconductors achieving reproducible responsivities up to 150 mA/W and photodiodes demonstrating detectivities achieving 6.5 × 10 10 Jones at 1200 nm with rapid response times, under zero applied bias.

papers.title: A near-infrared colloidal quantum dot imager with monolithically integrated readout circuitry
papers.sourceFile: /Users/shlokp/Zotero/storage/87CJH92Y/Liu et al. - 2022 - A near-infrared colloidal quantum dot imager with monolithically integrated readout circuitry.pdf

responsivity.evidence: Another imager for visible and short-wave infrared light, which used PbS CQD–graphene photodetectors 16, showed responsivity of 10 7 A W−1 and detectivity above 1012 Jones, but a low −3 dB bandwidth of around 0.16−1.60 kHz, large dark current and low frame imaging rate.
The responsivity and EQE are as high as 0.46 A W −1 and 63% at 970 nm, respectively, higher than many PbS CQD imagers20–22 at a similar wavelength17.

bandwidth.evidence: PbS CQDs have been reported to sensitize organic material for near-infrared imaging15, which were integrated onto thin-film transistor panels with 256 × 256 pixels and a pixel pitch of 154 μm, and exhibited a dark current density of 2.5 × 10−2 mA cm−2 at −5 V reverse bias, external quantum efficiency (EQE) of 16.5% and a −3 dB bandwidth of around 2.5 kHz.

Another imager for visible and short-wave infrared light, which used PbS CQD–graphene photodetectors 16, showed responsivity of 10 7 A W−1 and detectivity above 1012 Jones, but a low −3 dB bandwidth of around 0.16−1.60 kHz, large dark current and low frame imaging rate.

The colloidal quantum dot photodetector is designed with a structure compatible with complementary metal–oxide–semiconductors and exhibits a spectral range of 400–1,300 nm, room-temperature detectivity of 2.1 × 10 12 Jones, −3 dB bandwidth of 140 kHz and linear dynamic range of over 100 dB.
2d) suggests the fast response of the PbS CQD photodiode with a −3 dB bandwidth of up to 140 ± 3 kHz.
Our top-illuminated CQD photodiodes exhibit a specific detectivity of 2.1 × 10 12 Jones and a −3 dB bandwidth of 140 kHz.

The obtained stack based on CdSe/HgTe/ Ag 2 Te is then used to design a 2 μm cutoff wavelength photodiode with responsivity up to 0.8 A W −1 corresponding to an internal quantum efficiency of above 90%.

The device also achieves a high responsivity reaching 0.8 A W −1 , corresponding to an internal efficiency above 90% since the absorption is around 50% due to Fabry−Perot resonance within the diode stack.
